title: Blankney (mid-air collision) Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 7 crew killed; plus Hurricane PZ740 pilot Flg Off. Sidney Frederick Parlato, DFC

external_narratives.summary: ASN records the mid-air collision on 11 March 1945 between Lancaster LM130 of 463 Squadron RAAF (based at RAF Waddington) and Hurricane PZ740 of 1690 BDTF during a fighter affiliation exercise at night. All seven Lancaster crew were killed.
key_details: Lancaster piloted by P/O H. Orchard RAAF
external_narratives.notes: URL dead as of 2026-04-06; URL dead as of 2026-04-06

external_narratives.summary: ASN records Hurricane PZ740 of 1690 Flight piloted by Flg Off Sidney Frederick Parlato DFC RNZAF, who was also killed in the mid-air collision with Lancaster LM130.
external_narratives.notes: URL dead as of 2026-04-06; URL dead as of 2026-04-06

external_narratives.summary: IWM records the joint memorial for Lancaster LM130 and Hurricane PZ740 at Blankney, Lincolnshire, commemorating all eight personnel killed in the mid-air collision.
key_details: Joint memorial for both aircraft crews

external_narratives.summary: Photo album documenting the Blankney Fen memorial, showing a wooden cross with silhouettes of a Lancaster and Hurricane, located on a footpath from the Blankney Stepping Out car park.
key_details: Wooden cross memorial with aircraft silhouettes
Located on footpath from car park
Crash site was 'The Asholt Field' at Blankney village

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Photon geocoder reverse-confirmed current coords resolve to East Lindsey (16km NNE of Blankney)
Photon geocoder Blankney village centre confirmed 53.129 -0.4017
